frameworks.name: memory fidelity
frameworks.one_line: fidelity as a unifying dimension governing inter-item memory biases

frameworks.name: visual working memory interactions
frameworks.one_line: framework extended to encompass both attraction and repulsion biases

frameworks.name: attraction and repulsion effects
frameworks.one_line: phenomena from long-term memory and perception used for contextualization

core_question: What determines whether items in visual working memory show attraction versus repulsion biases toward one another?
key_assumptions: memory fidelity is a measurable, continuous dimension
subjective fidelity ratings track underlying memory precision
inter-item biases can be characterized on individual trials
manipulations: experimental manipulations of memory fidelity across three experiments
conditions designed to elicit attraction vs repulsion
key_findings: either attraction or repulsion can arise from manipulations of memory fidelity alone
subjective fidelity ratings predict attraction vs repulsion on a trial-by-trial basis
interpretations: memory fidelity provides a unifying dimension accommodating both bias types
results constrain computational models of visual working memory
